DiagnosisTopicRelevanceMention.description:
IBD diagnosis documentation: Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, IBD-unclassified,
indeterminate colitis, or active diagnostic workup for suspected IBD including VEOIBD.
How relevant is IBD diagnosis to the note for downstream processing?

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: an IBD diagnosis is stated, confirmed, or actively under evaluation (Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, IBD-unclassified, indeterminate colitis, or very early onset IBD); IMPLICIT: IBD is not named, but findings are consistent with new-onset or established IBD without a confirmed label (chronic bloody diarrhea workup, granulomas on biopsy, terminal ileitis on imaging, elevated fecal calprotectin, or endoscopic-pathologic correlation supporting IBD); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

DiagnosisTopicRelevanceMention.title: DiagnosisTopicRelevanceMention

EIMTopicRelevanceMention.description:
Extraintestinal Manifestations (EIM) of IBD.
How relevant are EIM findings to the note for downstream processing?
EIMs span musculoskeletal, dermatologic, hepatobiliary, and ocular organ systems.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: one or more EIMs are named or discussed in the context of IBD (arthritis, arthropathy, sacroiliitis, uveitis, episcleritis, erythema nodosum, pyoderma gangrenosum, aphthous stomatitis, primary sclerosing cholangitis, autoimmune hepatitis, nephrolithiasis, or thromboembolic disease); IMPLICIT: joint, skin, eye, or hepatobiliary findings are documented in a patient with known or suspected IBD, without explicit IBD attribution, but consistent with established EIM patterns; NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

EndoscopyTopicRelevanceMention.description:
IBD-relevant endoscopic procedure or findings.
Covers colonoscopy, sigmoidoscopy, ileoscopy, upper endoscopy, capsule endoscopy,
and balloon-assisted enteroscopy in the context of IBD assessment or monitoring.
How relevant are endoscopic findings to the note for downstream processing?

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: an endoscopic procedure is documented with IBD-relevant mucosal findings or used to assess IBD disease activity or mucosal healing (ulceration, friability, erythema, granularity, pseudopolyps, stricture, skip lesions, cobblestoning, or a formal endoscopic severity score); IMPLICIT: endoscopic findings are referenced in passing without a formal procedure report (e.g., 'prior scope showed ileitis', 'healing noted on last colonoscopy'), or an endoscopic procedure is planned or pending with IBD as the stated indication; NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

EndoscopyTopicRelevanceMention.title: EndoscopyTopicRelevanceMention

GeneticsTopicRelevanceMention.description:
IBD-relevant genetic evaluation: monogenic IBD workup, VEOIBD gene panels,
immune dysregulation syndromes, and IBD-associated gene variants.
How relevant is genetic information to the note for downstream processing?

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: genetic testing, a named gene variant, or a monogenic IBD syndrome is documented in the context of IBD evaluation (IL10, IL10RA, IL10RB, XIAP, CYBB, WAS, FOXP3, TTC7A, IPEX syndrome, chronic granulomatous disease, or a named IBD gene panel result); IMPLICIT: genetic evaluation is not named, but clinical features raise concern for a monogenic or immune dysregulation etiology warranting workup (IBD onset before age 6, consanguinity, family history of monogenic IBD, immunodeficiency features, or refractory infantile-onset colitis); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

GeneticsTopicRelevanceMention.title: GeneticsTopicRelevanceMention

MayoScoreTopicRelevanceMention.description:
Mayo Score for Ulcerative Colitis (full or partial).
How relevant is the Mayo Score assessment to the note for downstream processing?
The full Mayo Score includes an endoscopic subscore; the Partial Mayo Score is
clinician-reported only (stool frequency, rectal bleeding, physician global assessment).

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: Mayo Score, partial Mayo, Mayo endoscopic subscore, or Mayo subscore is named, scored, or directly referenced as a UC disease activity assessment; IMPLICIT: Mayo Score is not named, but ≥3 of its component domains are documentable in a UC context (stool frequency relative to normal, rectal bleeding severity, physician global assessment, or endoscopic appearance); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

MayoScoreTopicRelevanceMention.title: MayoScoreTopicRelevanceMention

MedicationsTopicRelevanceMention.description:
IBD-relevant medication documentation.
Covers aminosalicylates, corticosteroids, immunomodulators, biologics, and small molecule
therapies used for induction or maintenance of IBD remission, including monitoring context.
How relevant is medication information to the note for downstream processing?

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: one or more IBD-specific medication names or drug classes are explicitly mentioned (e.g., mesalamine, azathioprine, infliximab, vedolizumab, ustekinumab, budesonide, methotrexate, or JAK); IMPLICIT: medication management is referenced without naming a specific IBD drug or class (e.g., 'biologic therapy', 'immunosuppression', 'steroid taper', 'infusion therapy'); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

PCDAITopicRelevanceMention.description:
Pediatric Crohn's Disease Activity Index (PCDAI).
How relevant is the PCDAI assessment to the note for downstream processing?

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: PCDAI or one of its variants (aPCDAI, sPCDAI, wPCDAI) is named, scored, or directly referenced as an assessment; IMPLICIT: PCDAI is not named, but sufficient Crohn disease activity elements are documented to infer PCDAI relevance (abdominal pain, stooling pattern, general well-being or activity limitation, weight loss, abdominal exam findings, perirectal disease, extraintestinal manifestations, or inflammatory labs); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.

ParisClassificationTopicRelevanceMention.description:
Paris Classification of pediatric IBD: disease location, extent, behavior,
upper GI involvement, and growth impairment.
How relevant is Paris Classification documentation to the note for downstream processing?
The Paris Classification extends Montreal for pediatric patients, adding age at diagnosis,
upper GI extent descriptors, and growth impairment categories.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: Paris Classification descriptors are named or formally assigned (age at diagnosis A1a/A1b/A2, CD location L1–L4 with upper disease modifier, CD behavior B1–B3 with perianal modifier p, UC extent E1–E4, or growth impairment G0/G1); IMPLICIT: Paris Classification is not named, but pediatric IBD phenotype elements are documented in sufficient detail to assign a class (anatomic distribution on endoscopy or imaging, stricturing or penetrating behavior, perianal involvement, upper GI involvement, or growth parameter assessment); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

SESCDTopicRelevanceMention.description:
Simple Endoscopic Score for Crohn's Disease (SES-CD).
How relevant is the SES-CD assessment to the note for downstream processing?
SES-CD scores four endoscopic domains across five ileocolonic segments.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: SES-CD is named, scored, or directly referenced as an endoscopic severity assessment in a Crohn's disease context; IMPLICIT: SES-CD is not named, but ≥2 of its 4 scored domains are described across one or more ileocolonic segments (ulcer size, ulcerated surface proportion, affected surface proportion, or luminal narrowing/stenosis); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

StoolingTopicRelevanceMention.description:
IBD-relevant stooling pattern documentation.
Covers frequency, consistency, blood or mucus content, nocturnal stools, urgency,
tenesmus, and incontinence as markers of intestinal disease activity.
How relevant is stooling documentation to the note for downstream processing?

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: stooling pattern is formally documented with quantitative or semi-quantitative detail relevant to IBD activity assessment (stool frequency per day, Bristol Stool Scale type, presence and grade of rectal bleeding, mucus in stool, nocturnal awakening due to bowel urgency, tenesmus, or fecal incontinence); IMPLICIT: stooling is referenced qualitatively without structured documentation but carries IBD-relevant information ('loose stools', 'blood in stool', 'going more frequently', 'diarrhea improved on current regimen', 'no nocturnal symptoms'); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

SurgeryTopicRelevanceMention.description:
IBD-related surgical history, planning, or post-operative documentation.
Covers bowel resection, colectomy, ostomy, strictureplasty, drainage procedures,
and perianal surgery.
How relevant is surgical information to the note for downstream processing?

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: Verbatim text fragments from the note that most strongly support the classification. Must be empty when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.
spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: an IBD-related surgical procedure is named, planned, or documented as part of the patient's history or current management plan (ileocolic resection, subtotal colectomy, proctocolectomy, ileal pouch-anal anastomosis, ileostomy, colostomy, strictureplasty, fistulotomy, seton placement, drainage procedure, or diverting ostomy); IMPLICIT: surgery is not named, but clinical indicators strongly suggest prior or planned IBD-related surgery (surgical referral for medically refractory disease, stoma care or ostomy output documentation without naming the procedure, or post-operative anastomosis or wound management notes); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.

UCEISTopicRelevanceMention.description:
Ulcerative Colitis Endoscopic Index of Severity (UCEIS).
How relevant is the UCEIS assessment to the note for downstream processing?
UCEIS is an endoscopy-only instrument with three domains: vascular pattern,
bleeding, and erosions/ulcers.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.

spans.title: Spans

relevance.description: EXPLICIT: UCEIS is named, scored, or directly referenced as an endoscopic severity assessment in a UC context; IMPLICIT: UCEIS is not named, but ≥2 of its 3 endoscopic component domains are documented in a UC context (mucosal vascular pattern, bleeding source and severity, erosions or ulcer characteristics); NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: not enough evidence.

confidence.description: Confidence in the relevance classification from 0.0 to 1.0. When relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE, this reflects confidence in absence, not presence.
confidence.title: Confidence

reasoning.description: One concise sentence explaining why this relevance level was assigned and why a higher or lower level was not chosen. Use None when relevance is NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE.

UCEISTopicRelevanceMention.title: UCEISTopicRelevanceMention

description:
Top-level relevance gate for a single clinical note.
Each field classifies one IBD concept's presence before downstream extraction.
Fields where is_relevant=True are routed to their respective extraction pipelines.
Fields where relevance=IMPLICIT may warrant reconstruction pipelines depending on the concept.
Add one field per concept as new TopicRelevanceMention subclasses are defined.
title: TopicRelevanceAnnotation
